D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Sine-Grating MTF — Modulation vs Spatial Frequency

MTF(f) is the ratio of output modulation to input modulation as a function of spatial frequency; declines from 1 at DC to 0 at the cutoff frequency (2 NA / λ)

Imaging system MTF is the Fourier transform of the PSF. For a circular aperture: MTF(f) = (2/π)·[cos⁻¹(f/f_c) − (f/f_c)·√(1 − (f/f_c)²)]. Lens reviews quote MTF50 (frequency at 50% contrast) — a single number summary of sharpness.

Slide spatial frequency to compare input grating and output contrast.
